title: Document herdr-go service subcommand in README and installation docs
action: Update README.md and docs/installation.md to document the herdr-go service {start|stop|restart|status} subcommand built in the sibling cell as the recommended unified way to control the service, while keeping the existing raw per-platform OS commands (systemctl/launchctl/Scheduled Task) documented too as a fallback/reference (per D8). README.md's existing ### Restart section (README.md:61-71) should cover all four verbs, not just restart; docs/installation.md's restart block (docs/installation.md:69-76) and its scattered start mentions (docs/installation.md:17-19) should be consolidated similarly. Exact heading/section layout is left to your judgment as long as both requirements hold: the new command is documented as primary, and the old commands stay documented as reference.

truths: README.md documents herdr-go service start, stop, restart, and status for all three platforms
docs/installation.md documents the same four verbs for all three platforms
Neither doc deletes the existing raw systemctl/launchctl/Scheduled Task commands

artifacts.substantive: service management section covers all 4 verbs plus the herdr-go service command

key_links: README.md and docs/installation.md stay consistent with each other
prohibitions: Do not remove the existing raw per-platform OS commands from either doc

verify: grep -q 'herdr-go service' README.md && grep -q 'herdr-go service' docs/installation.md && grep -q 'systemctl --user' README.md && grep -q 'systemctl --user' docs/installation.md && echo DOCS_OK

trace.outcome: Documented herdr-go service {start|stop|restart|status} as the recommended unified command in README.md and docs/installation.md, with a per-platform (launchctl/systemctl/Scheduled Task) reference table kept alongside as fallback; fixed a stale cross-reference to the renamed Restart heading.
trace.verify_command: grep -q 'herdr-go service' README.md && grep -q 'herdr-go service' docs/installation.md && grep -q 'systemctl --user' README.md && grep -q 'systemctl --user' docs/installation.md && echo DOCS_OK
